{"id":"project-LICHEN-avlh","title":"announce: SchedulerConfig allows negative jitter_ms causing ValueError at runtime","description":"File: python/src/lichen/announce/scheduler.py:257

SchedulerConfig does not validate that jitter_ms >= 0:

```python
@dataclass
class SchedulerConfig:
    jitter_ms: int = DEFAULT_JITTER_MS  # No validation
```

In _loop(), this causes a runtime error:

```python
jitter = random.randint(0, self.config.jitter_ms)  # Line 257
```

If jitter_ms is negative (e.g., -1), random.randint(0, -1) raises:
  ValueError: empty range for randrange()

While the catch-all exception handler prevents a crash, it logs an error and retries every second, spamming logs indefinitely.

Fix: Add validation in SchedulerConfig.__post_init__ or in AnnounceScheduler before using the config.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:25.812074Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:25.812111Z"}
